title: Netflix Officially Confirms Fate of Duffer Brothers' 'Stranger Things' Replacement

blocks.content: Stranger Things was a staple of streaming service Netflix for nearly a decade, as it debuted in 2016 and, after five seasons, finally concluded last year on Dec. 31, 2025. Based on the program's success almost immediately after it debuted, it inspired countless imitators, whether new projects focused on young protagonists, were set in a nostalgic timeline, or blended together horror, sci-fi, and action-adventure tones. Even though the proper series has concluded, the show's influence will likely be felt for years to come.

Matt and Ross Duffer created Stranger Things and the show's success allowed them to develop a number of other projects. While The Boroughs was created by Jeffrey Addiss and Will Matthews, the Duffer brothers served as executive producers. Their influence and cinematic eye could be heavily felt, and the release of The Boroughs helped ease the pain of no longer having proper Stranger Things episodes to watch.

As confirmed today by Deadline, after only one season, The Boroughs has been canceled on Netflix. News of the cancellation comes as a surprise for a number of reasons. Netflix is known for having deep pockets to fund the most expensive of movies and TV shows, but the outlet claims the sci-fi nature of the show meant it was a more expensive endeavor than other titles, and the initial viewership of The Boroughs failed to justify a renewal. Even more surprising is that a writers' room had been assembled for a Season 2, with some reports claiming that Season 2 and 3 could have been filmed simultaneously.

The Boroughs stars Alfred Molina, Geena Davis, Alfre Woodard, Denis O’Hare, Clarke Peters, and Bill Pullman as seniors who notice some strange things happening in their neighborhood. Rather than just accept this strangeness, they investigate the events, similar to our protagonists in Stranger Things.

Even though the viewership didn't reflect it, The Boroughs premiered to impressive reviews. The show currently sits at 97% positive reviews on aggregator site Rotten Tomatoes, which mirrors the debut season of Stranger Things and surpasses all subsequent seasons. In addition to the costly nature of the series, it's also possible that, with the Duffer Brothers striking a deal with Paramount, Netflix was less inclined to continue that collaboration. However, there's another season of the spin-off Stranger Things: Tales from '85 on the way, as well as a live-action spin-off.

This marks the second single-season horror show from the filmmakers this year, as they also produced Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen. That series, though, was approached as a limited series as opposed to offering an open-ended conclusion.
Found an error? Send it info@movieweb.com so it can be corrected.

In the retirement community of The Boroughs, newcomer Sam Cooper uncovers a terrifying secret hidden beneath its tranquil desert setting. Ignored by those in charge, he teams up with a group of underestimated residents to investigate a deadly force stalking the neighborhood.
